title: ononis antennata subsp. massesylia
intro: Ononis antennata subsp. massesylia, also known as the sweet broom, is a perennial legume plant native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of this plant have been utilized for its astringent and diuretic effects, helping to treat various health conditions such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Ononis antennata subsp. massesylia include its ability to relieve inflammation, reduce fever, and aid in digestion, making it a valuable addition to traditional herbal remedies. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared as an infusion or decoction to utilize its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and applications.
